tasks.title: Improve Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) Performance
tasks.description: Optimize the application to reduce Cumulative Layout Shift by implementing proper image sizing, font loading strategies, and element pre-allocation to improve Core Web Vitals.
tasks.details: 1. **Analyze Current CLS Issues**: Use browser DevTools and Lighthouse to identify specific elements causing layout shifts, focusing on recipe cards, images, and dynamic content loading. 2. **Implement Image Optimization**: Add explicit width/height attributes to all images, implement responsive image sizing with aspect-ratio CSS, and use placeholder elements for lazy-loaded content in recipe cards and galleries. 3. **Optimize Font Loading**: Implement font-display: swap for custom fonts, preload critical fonts in the HTML head, and use font-size-adjust to minimize layout shifts during font loading. 4. **Pre-allocate Dynamic Content**: Reserve space for dynamically loaded content like recipe cards, search results, and pagination components by using skeleton screens or fixed-height containers. 5. **Optimize Component Mounting**: Ensure Web Components in Shadow DOM don't cause layout shifts during initialization by pre-defining container dimensions and using CSS transforms instead of changing layout properties. 6. **Implement Proper Loading States**: Add consistent loading placeholders that match final content dimensions for all async operations including Firebase data fetching and image loading.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. **CLS Measurement**: Use Lighthouse CI to measure CLS scores before and after optimizations, aiming for a score below 0.1. Run tests on multiple page types including home, categories, and individual recipe pages. 2. **Visual Regression Testing**: Capture screenshots during page load to verify no unexpected layout shifts occur during content loading phases. 3. **Performance Testing**: Test on slow network connections (3G simulation) to ensure optimizations work under real-world conditions. 4. **Component Testing**: Verify that each Web Component loads without causing layout shifts by testing Shadow DOM mounting and content initialization. 5. **Cross-Browser Testing**: Test CLS improvements across different browsers and device sizes to ensure consistent performance gains.

tasks.title: Comprehensive Testing Suite for All Pages and Components
tasks.description: Implement extensive automated and manual testing coverage for all application pages, components, and user interactions to ensure quality and reliability.
tasks.details: 1. **Component Testing Setup**: Establish a comprehensive testing framework using Jest and Testing Library to test all Web Components in isolation, including Shadow DOM testing capabilities and custom element registration verification. 2. **Page-Level Integration Testing**: Create integration tests for all major pages (home, categories, recipes, user profile) that verify complete user workflows, navigation, data loading, and responsive behavior across different screen sizes. 3. **Visual Regression Testing**: Implement visual testing using tools like Playwright or Chromatic to capture screenshots of all pages and components, ensuring UI consistency across browsers and preventing unintended visual changes. 4. **Performance Testing**: Add automated performance tests that verify Core Web Vitals metrics (LCP, FID, CLS) for all pages, with specific attention to recipe loading times and image optimization effectiveness. 5. **Accessibility Testing**: Implement automated accessibility testing using axe-core to verify WCAG compliance across all components, including keyboard navigation, screen reader compatibility, and proper ARIA attributes. 6. **Cross-Browser Testing**: Set up automated testing across major browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge) to ensure consistent functionality and appearance. 7. **Mobile Testing**: Create specific test suites for mobile interactions, touch events, and responsive design breakpoints. 8. **API Integration Testing**: Test all data fetching, recipe CRUD operations, user authentication flows, and error handling scenarios.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. **Unit Test Coverage**: Achieve minimum 90% code coverage for all components and utilities, with specific focus on edge cases and error conditions. 2. **End-to-End Testing**: Create comprehensive E2E test suites that cover complete user journeys including recipe browsing, searching, creating, editing, and user account management. 3. **Test Data Management**: Establish proper test data fixtures and mocking strategies for consistent and reliable test execution. 4. **Continuous Integration**: Integrate all tests into CI/CD pipeline with automated test execution on pull requests and deployments. 5. **Performance Benchmarks**: Set up automated performance monitoring that fails builds if Core Web Vitals thresholds are exceeded. 6. **Manual Testing Checklist**: Create comprehensive manual testing checklists for features that require human validation, including UX flows and edge case scenarios.

tasks.title: Replace Font Awesome CDN with local npm package
tasks.description: Install @fortawesome/fontawesome-free as a local dependency and replace CDN imports to improve performance and enable offline functionality.
tasks.details:
1. Install @fortawesome/fontawesome-free package via npm
2. Remove CDN import from filter-manager-styles.js and any other files
3. Import Font Awesome CSS locally in the appropriate bundler configuration or main CSS file
4. Update Shadow DOM components to properly load Font Awesome styles within shadow boundaries
5. Verify all existing Font Awesome icons continue to display correctly
6. Test icon rendering in both connected and offline environments
7. Update build process to include Font Awesome assets in the final bundle
8. Consider using CSS custom properties or CSS-in-JS approach for Shadow DOM icon styling if needed
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Verify all existing Font Awesome icons render correctly across all components
2. Test application functionality in offline mode to ensure icons still display
3. Run performance tests to confirm improved loading times without CDN dependency
4. Check Shadow DOM components specifically for proper icon rendering
5. Validate that no console errors occur related to missing Font Awesome resources
6. Test on different browsers to ensure cross-browser compatibility
7. Verify build process includes all necessary Font Awesome assets

tasks.title: Enhance Propose Recipe Form with Data Persistence and Navigation Protection
tasks.description: Implement form field persistence on page refresh and prevent accidental navigation when form contains data to improve user experience.
tasks.details:
1. Implement localStorage/sessionStorage to automatically save form field values as user types
2. Add form state restoration on page load to repopulate saved field values
3. Implement beforeunload event listener to detect unsaved changes and show confirmation dialog
4. Add form dirty state tracking to determine if form has been modified
5. Create utility functions for form data serialization/deserialization
6. Handle edge cases like form submission (clear saved data) and intentional navigation
7. Consider implementing debounced saving to optimize performance
8. Ensure compatibility with existing form validation and submission logic
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Fill out form fields, refresh page, verify all field values are restored
2. Fill out form, attempt to navigate away, confirm warning dialog appears
3. Submit form successfully, verify saved data is cleared from storage
4. Test with various field types (text, textarea, select, file uploads)
5. Verify performance with rapid typing (debounced saving works correctly)
6. Test browser back/forward navigation with unsaved changes
7. Verify functionality works across different browsers and devices

subtasks.title: Implement form data persistence utilities
subtasks.description: Create utility functions for saving and loading form data to/from localStorage with debounced saving to optimize performance
subtasks.details: Develop core utilities including: saveFormData() function with debounced saving (300ms delay), loadFormData() function to retrieve saved data, clearFormData() function to remove saved data, and data serialization/deserialization helpers. Include error handling for localStorage unavailability and implement namespacing to avoid conflicts with other app data.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for each utility function covering save/load/clear operations, debouncing behavior, error handling when localStorage is disabled, and data serialization accuracy

subtasks.title: Add form dirty state tracking system
subtasks.description: Implement a system to track whether the form has been modified from its initial state
subtasks.details: Create a form dirty state tracker that monitors form field changes, maintains initial form values for comparison, provides isDirty() method to check if form has unsaved changes, and integrates with existing form validation. Track changes on input, textarea, and select elements with proper event handling.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for dirty state detection covering initial state, field modifications, form reset scenarios, and edge cases like programmatic value changes

subtasks.title: Implement automatic form field persistence
subtasks.description: Add real-time saving of form field values as user types using the persistence utilities and dirty state tracking
subtasks.details: Integrate form field persistence by attaching debounced save handlers to form inputs, implementing automatic saving on blur events as backup, excluding sensitive fields from persistence if any, and ensuring compatibility with existing form components. Handle different input types (text, number, select, checkbox, etc.).
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration tests covering automatic saving during typing, persistence across different input types, debouncing behavior, and exclusion of sensitive fields

subtasks.title: Add form state restoration on page load
subtasks.description: Implement automatic restoration of saved form field values when the page loads or component mounts
subtasks.details: Create form restoration logic that loads saved data on component mount, populates form fields with saved values, handles cases where saved data structure doesn't match current form, and integrates with existing form initialization. Ensure proper handling of form validation after restoration.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration tests for form restoration covering successful data loading, handling of missing or corrupted saved data, form validation after restoration, and compatibility with existing form initialization

subtasks.title: Implement navigation protection with beforeunload warning
subtasks.description: Add beforeunload event listener to warn users about unsaved changes and handle form submission cleanup
subtasks.details: Implement navigation protection using beforeunload event listener that shows confirmation dialog when form has unsaved changes, automatically clears saved data on successful form submission, handles intentional navigation scenarios, and provides user-friendly warning messages. Ensure proper cleanup of event listeners.
subtasks.testStrategy: End-to-end tests covering navigation warnings with unsaved changes, no warning when form is clean, automatic cleanup on form submission, and proper event listener management

tasks.title: Add Recipe Image Suggestion Feature with Approval Workflow
tasks.description: Implement a comprehensive image suggestion system for recipes that includes entry points from recipe views, manager approval workflow, and image proposal components.
tasks.details:
1. Create recipe image suggestion entry points:
   - Add 'Suggest Image' button/link on recipe detail pages
   - Add image suggestion option in recipe edit forms
   - Implement context-aware entry points based on recipe completion status

2. Build image proposal component:
   - Create form for users to submit image URLs or upload files
   - Add image preview functionality with cropping/resizing options
   - Implement validation for image formats, sizes, and quality
   - Add fields for image attribution/source information
   - Include recipe association and tagging capabilities

3. Implement manager approval system:
   - Create admin dashboard for pending image suggestions
   - Build approval/rejection workflow with comments
   - Add notification system for suggestion status updates
   - Implement batch approval functionality for efficiency
   - Create audit trail for all approval decisions

4. Database schema updates:
   - Create image_suggestions table with fields for recipe_id, user_id, image_url, status, submitted_at, reviewed_at, reviewer_id
   - Add indexes for efficient querying by recipe and status
   - Implement soft delete for maintaining history

5. Integration points:
   - Connect with existing recipe management system
   - Integrate with user authentication and role management
   - Link with image storage and CDN services
   - Add analytics tracking for suggestion metrics
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test recipe image suggestion entry points:
   - Verify 'Suggest Image' buttons appear on all recipe pages
   - Test suggestion form accessibility from recipe edit pages
   - Confirm proper recipe context is maintained through workflow

2. Test image proposal component:
   - Upload various image formats and verify validation
   - Test image preview and cropping functionality
   - Verify form submission with all required fields
   - Test error handling for invalid images or network issues

3. Test manager approval workflow:
   - Submit suggestions and verify they appear in admin dashboard
   - Test approval and rejection flows with comment functionality
   - Verify notification system sends appropriate status updates
   - Test batch approval with multiple suggestions

4. Integration testing:
   - Verify proper user authentication and authorization
   - Test database operations for all CRUD operations
   - Confirm image storage and retrieval functionality
   - Test cross-browser compatibility and mobile responsiveness

5. End-to-end testing:
   - Complete full workflow from suggestion to approval
   - Test user permissions and role-based access control
   - Verify analytics tracking captures all events correctly

tasks.title: Add Media Instructions to Recipes - Proposal, Presentation and Suggestion Features
tasks.description: Implement comprehensive media instruction functionality including adding media to recipe proposals, displaying media in recipe presentation, and providing options to suggest new instruction media.
tasks.details:
1. Extend recipe proposal form with media instruction capabilities:
   - Add media upload/URL input fields for each instruction step
   - Implement drag-and-drop media upload interface
   - Add media type validation (images, videos, GIFs)
   - Create media preview components with edit/remove options
   - Integrate with existing form persistence system

2. Enhance recipe presentation with media display:
   - Create responsive media display components for instruction steps
   - Implement media carousel/gallery for multiple media per step
   - Add media zoom/fullscreen functionality
   - Optimize media loading and lazy loading for performance
   - Ensure proper media fallbacks and error handling

3. Build media suggestion system for existing recipes:
   - Create 'Suggest Media for Instructions' entry points on recipe pages
   - Implement step-specific media suggestion forms
   - Add media suggestion approval workflow integration
   - Create suggestion tracking and status display
   - Link with existing image suggestion infrastructure

4. Database and API enhancements:
   - Extend recipe instruction schema to include media fields
   - Create media suggestion API endpoints
   - Implement media storage and CDN integration
   - Add media metadata tracking (dimensions, file size, type)

5. User interface improvements:
   - Create consistent media interaction patterns
   - Add media accessibility features (alt text, captions)
   - Implement media compression and optimization
   - Design mobile-responsive media interfaces
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test recipe proposal media functionality:
   - Upload various media types during recipe creation
   - Verify media persistence across form sessions
   - Test media validation and error handling
   - Confirm media preview and edit capabilities

2. Test recipe presentation media display:
   - View recipes with instruction media on different devices
   - Test media carousel navigation and controls
   - Verify media zoom and fullscreen functionality
   - Test media loading performance and fallbacks

3. Test media suggestion workflow:
   - Suggest media for existing recipe instructions
   - Verify suggestion forms are step-specific and contextual
   - Test suggestion submission and approval process
   - Confirm suggestion tracking and status updates

4. Test cross-device compatibility:
   - Verify media upload works on mobile devices
   - Test media display responsiveness
   - Confirm touch interactions work properly

5. Test accessibility and performance:
   - Verify alt text and caption functionality
   - Test media loading optimization
   - Confirm keyboard navigation support
   - Test with screen readers and accessibility tools

tasks.title: Implement Avatar Image Fallback to Icon
tasks.description: Create a fallback mechanism where avatar images automatically display a default icon when the user image fails to load or is unavailable.
tasks.details: Implement an avatar component that handles image loading errors gracefully. When a user's profile image fails to load (404, network error, or missing src), the component should automatically display a default user icon instead of a broken image placeholder. This involves: 1) Creating or updating an Avatar component with onError handling, 2) Adding a default user icon (could be SVG or icon font), 3) Implementing proper error state management, 4) Ensuring the fallback icon maintains consistent styling with avatar dimensions, 5) Adding loading states if needed, 6) Testing various failure scenarios including slow networks and missing images.
tasks.testStrategy: Test avatar fallback by: 1) Providing invalid image URLs and verifying icon appears, 2) Testing with slow network to ensure loading states work, 3) Testing with valid images to ensure normal display, 4) Verifying icon styling matches avatar container dimensions, 5) Testing across different screen sizes and devices, 6) Checking accessibility with screen readers, 7) Testing rapid switching between valid and invalid image sources.

tasks.title: Update Firebase Security Rules and Data Model for Media Instructions
tasks.description: Modify Firebase Storage security rules to allow authenticated users to upload/delete media for recipes they own. Define and enforce the data structure for the `mediaInstructions` array within Firestore.
tasks.details:
In `firebase.rules` (or equivalent), update the storage rules for the `recipes/{recipeId}/media-instructions/` path. Implement the `isValidMediaFile` and `userOwnsRecipe` functions as specified in the PRD. Ensure public read access is enabled. This task lays the security and data foundation for all subsequent tasks.
<info added on 2025-10-18T20:16:37.796Z>
### Critical Safety Notes for Dual Firebase Environment

**SAFETY REQUIREMENTS:**
- Export existing rules from Firebase Console FIRST before making any changes
- Create local `storage.rules` and `firestore.rules` files
- Use separate storage path: `recipes/{recipeId}/media-instructions/` (don't touch `img/recipes/*`)
- Deploy to STAGING first (`cook-book-test-479e8`)
- Test for 24-48 hours in staging
- Only deploy to PRODUCTION (`my-cook-book-67fde`) after staging verification
- Must maintain backward compatibility - don't break existing `img/recipes/*` rules

**TESTING CHECKLIST:**
- Test upload with valid image/video
- Test upload >50MB (should fail)
- Test unauthenticated upload (should fail)
- Test upload to recipe you don't own (should fail)
- CRITICAL: Verify existing recipes still work
</info added on 2025-10-18T20:16:37.796Z>
tasks.testStrategy: Use the Firebase Rules Playground to simulate read, create, and delete operations for authenticated recipe owners and non-owners. Test with valid and invalid file sizes/types to ensure rules are enforced correctly. Verify no regressions are introduced to existing rules.

tasks.title: Create Media Instruction Utility Functions
tasks.description: Develop a set of utility functions to handle the lifecycle of media instruction files, including uploading to Firebase Storage, deleting from storage, and data validation.
tasks.details:
Create a new file at `src/lib/utils/recipes/recipe-media-utils.js`. Implement three key functions: `uploadMediaInstructionFile(file, recipeId, userId)` which uploads a file to `recipes/{recipeId}/media-instructions/` and returns its metadata; `deleteMediaInstructionFile(filePath)` to remove a file from storage; and `validateMediaInstructionData(mediaInstructions)` which can be used client-side before submission. These functions will abstract the Firebase Storage logic.
<info added on 2025-10-18T20:17:03.642Z>
### Implementation Notes:
- **Storage Path**: Confirm all media instruction files are stored under `recipes/{recipeId}/media-instructions/`. Ensure no operations interfere with the legacy `img/recipes/*` path.
- **Image Compression**: For uploaded image files, apply server-side compression using the `sharp` library, mirroring the pattern found in `functions/index.js`. Images should be resized to a maximum of 800x600 pixels (maintaining aspect ratio) and compressed with a JPEG quality of 80.
- **Error Handling**: Implement robust error handling for all utility functions, gracefully managing network failures, invalid file types, and other potential issues during upload, deletion, or validation.
- **Backward Compatibility**: The new media instruction upload system must operate independently and not cause any regressions or interference with the existing `img/recipes/*` image upload and display mechanisms.
- **Dependencies**: This task is critically dependent on Task #1 (Firebase security rules and data model for media instructions). Task #1's changes must be deployed to the staging environment and verified before proceeding with the deployment of these utility functions.
- **Testing Environment**: All development and testing should initially target the `.env.staging` environment to prevent unintended side effects on production data.
</info added on 2025-10-18T20:17:03.642Z>
tasks.testStrategy: Write unit tests for each utility function. For `uploadMediaInstructionFile`, mock the Firebase Storage API and verify it's called with the correct path and file. For `deleteMediaInstructionFile`, test that the correct delete command is issued. For `validateMediaInstructionData`, test with valid and malformed data arrays.

tasks.title: Develop the Media Instructions Editor Svelte Component
tasks.description: Create a new Svelte component for creating, editing, reordering, and deleting media instructions within the recipe form.
tasks.details:
Create `src/lib/recipes/media-instructions/MediaInstructionsEditor.svelte`. This component will manage an array of media instruction objects. It should feature: a file input with drag-and-drop support; a text input for captions (supporting Hebrew); a delete button for each item; and drag handles for reordering using the existing `svelte-dnd-action` library. It will use the utility functions from Task 2 for file operations. The component should emit events for data changes.
<info added on 2025-10-18T20:17:46.291Z>
### Implementation Notes:
- **INTEGRATION PATTERNS:** Follow recent form refactoring patterns from development branch. This likely refers to patterns found in `src/lib/components/recipes/RecipeForm.svelte` or related files, as indicated by Task 4.
- **RTL SUPPORT:** Hebrew captions require proper `text-align: right` and potentially `direction: rtl` for correct display, given the project's focus on Hebrew content.
- **REUSABILITY:** Consider reusing sectioned-list patterns if applicable. This might involve looking at existing list components or patterns in `src/lib/components/shared/` or other recipe-related components.
- **VALIDATION:** Use consistent validation patterns with other form components. This implies using the `zod` schema extension pattern as seen in Task 4's description for `src/lib/utils/recipes.js`.

### Dependencies:
- Requires rebase onto development first (to get latest form patterns).
- Requires Task #2 (Create Media Instruction Utility Functions) to be completed, as this component will utilize the `uploadMediaInstructionFile`, `deleteMediaInstructionFile`, and `validateMediaInstructionData` functions from `src/lib/utils/recipes/recipe-media-utils.js`.
</info added on 2025-10-18T20:17:46.291Z>
tasks.testStrategy: Use Storybook or a similar component testing environment to develop this component in isolation. Test file selection, caption input, item deletion, and drag-and-drop reordering. Verify that the component's internal state correctly reflects user actions.

tasks.title: Integrate Media Editor into the Recipe Form
tasks.description: Embed the new `MediaInstructionsEditor` component into the main recipe form and connect it to the recipe's data model and validation system.
tasks.details:
Modify the existing recipe form component (e.g., `src/lib/components/recipes/RecipeForm.svelte`). Add a new section titled 'הוראות הכנה מצולמות'. Import and render the `MediaInstructionsEditor.svelte` component, binding its data to the recipe's `mediaInstructions` field. Extend the existing `zod` validation schema in `src/lib/utils/recipes.js` to validate the `mediaInstructions` array according to the PRD's data structure.
<info added on 2025-10-18T20:18:22.371Z>
Ensure backward compatibility: The `mediaInstructions` field must be optional in the recipe data model. Update the `zod` validation schema in `src/lib/utils/recipes.js` to reflect this, using `z.array(...).optional()`. The implementation must not break existing recipes that do not have `mediaInstructions` data. The testing strategy should include verifying that recipes can be saved both with and without media instructions, and that existing recipes load correctly without any changes.
</info added on 2025-10-18T20:18:22.371Z>
tasks.testStrategy: Perform integration testing on the recipe edit page. Verify that media instructions can be added and are correctly saved with the recipe. Test the form's validation by attempting to save with invalid data (e.g., missing caption if required). Ensure that loading an existing recipe correctly populates the editor.

tasks.title: Enhance MediaScroller Component for Rich Content Display
tasks.description: Upgrade the existing `MediaScroller.svelte` component to support captions, different media types (image/video), and click events for fullscreen viewing.
tasks.details: Refactor `src/lib/components/shared/MediaScroller.svelte`. Modify its `items` prop to accept an array of objects matching the `mediaInstructions` structure (`path`, `caption`, `type`). Add a slot or prop for displaying the caption below the media. Implement logic to render an `` or `
tasks.testStrategy: Update the component's unit tests or stories to pass in the new data structure. Verify that both images and videos render correctly. Test that captions are displayed and that the `itemclick` event is dispatched with the correct payload upon user interaction.

tasks.title: Implement Fullscreen Media Viewer Modal
tasks.description: Create a reusable modal component for displaying a single media instruction in fullscreen with navigation controls.
tasks.details: Create a new component `src/lib/components/shared/FullscreenMediaViewer.svelte`. This modal should accept an array of media items and the index of the currently active item. It should display the media prominently, show the caption, and include 'Previous'/'Next' buttons and keyboard navigation (arrow keys) to cycle through the items. Include a close button (and Esc key listener) to exit the modal.
tasks.testStrategy: Test the modal component in isolation. Verify it displays the correct image/video and caption based on the props. Test the 'Previous' and 'Next' buttons, ensuring they correctly cycle through the items and handle edge cases (first/last item). Test all close mechanisms (button, Esc key, overlay click).

tasks.title: Integrate Media Display into the Recipe View Page
tasks.description: Update the recipe display page to render the media instructions using the enhanced `MediaScroller` and `FullscreenMediaViewer` components.
tasks.details:
In the recipe display route (e.g., `src/routes/recipes/[slug]/+page.svelte`), add a new collapsible section for 'הוראות הכנה מצולמות'. If `recipe.mediaInstructions` exists, render the enhanced `MediaScroller` component, passing the data to it. Implement lazy loading for this section. Listen for the `itemclick` event from the scroller to open the `FullscreenMediaViewer` modal with the appropriate data.
<info added on 2025-10-18T20:18:31.032Z>
Test that the collapsible section for 'הוראות הכנה מצולמות' only appears if `recipe.mediaInstructions` exists and has items. Verify that recipes without the `mediaInstructions` field or with an empty array still display correctly without any errors or visual regressions. Confirm that the lazy loading mechanism prevents any performance impact on recipes that do not have media instructions. Ensure that the display and functionality of existing recipe details (those not related to media instructions) remain identical to their pre-implementation state.
</info added on 2025-10-18T20:18:31.032Z>
tasks.testStrategy: Conduct end-to-end testing on a recipe that has media instructions. Verify the media scroller displays correctly on both desktop and mobile. Confirm that clicking an item opens the fullscreen modal. Test navigation within the modal. Check that the section is properly collapsed by default and loads media only upon expansion.

tasks.title: Implement Performance Optimizations and Sanitization
tasks.description: Apply performance best practices such as image compression and lazy loading, and ensure all user-generated content is properly sanitized.
tasks.details: Integrate an image compression library or a cloud function to automatically compress images on upload to reduce storage costs and improve load times. Ensure the `MediaScroller` on the recipe view page lazy-loads media content. Review all points where `mediaInstructions` data (especially `caption`) is rendered and ensure it is sanitized to prevent XSS attacks, possibly using a DOMPurify-like library or Svelte's built-in mechanisms.
tasks.testStrategy: Use browser developer tools (Lighthouse, Network tab) to measure recipe page load times before and after implementation to ensure no performance degradation. Manually test for XSS vulnerabilities by entering HTML/script tags into caption fields. Verify that images are being compressed by checking file sizes in Firebase Storage.
